betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §5§lMiniature Wormhole Generator§r uses Quantum Entangled Singularities to transmit power over any distance, including across dimensions.

To use it, you must link two generators together using AE2 Entangled Singularities by placing one in each controller. Once linked, both wormholes must be started by placing a full (non-entangled) AE2 Singularity into an input bus on each machine. The wormhole will continue running as long as power is being transferred between them. If power transfer stops, the link will shut down and must be restarted using another Singularity on both ends.

Power is transferred through Laser Source Hatches and Laser Target Hatches. Each wormhole can support up to 4 laser hatches. These must be placed in the center block of one of the side faces, excluding the controller side. Hatch pairs link based on relative position between the two wormholes, not cardinal direction. For example, a Source Hatch placed on the top of the sending side must connect to a Target Hatch placed on the bottom of the receiving wormhole.

Additionally, unlike AE2 P2P tunnels, this does not come with a 5% power loss but rather a much more manageable 0.5% loss. 

[note]The glass and input hatch must be tiered accordingly with the tier of the laser hatches. But remember, there is no amperage limit on this machine, so use whatever makes it cheapest for you.[/note]
